text_evidence: This is the post-test disassemble process of this reactor.
The reactor was operated with a 3-micrometer metal chamber containing circulating 1-molecule
The reactor was operated with a 3-micrometer metal chamber containing circulating 1-molecule H-electrolytes.
After the electrochemical test, we will disassemble the cell and record the conditions of each
After the electrochemical test, we will disassemble the cell and record the conditions of each component for failure diagonal.
Now we load the 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence to release the compressions regularly to avoid
Now we load the 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence to release the compressions regularly to avoid
damaging the membrane or electrodes.

triple: first inspect the anode flow plate
text_evidence: After opening the reactor, we first inspect the anode flow plate.
We check whether there is liquid accumulation, salt precipitation, gas channel blockage,
We check whether there is liquid accumulation, salt precipitation, gas channel blockage,
We check whether there is liquid accumulation, salt precipitation, gas channel blockage,
or any color change on the flow field.
The anode is Pt-RuCM-CubSpR-CoT on a H23C3 carbon paper.
We check whether the catalyst layer remains intact, whether the surface is flooded, whether
We check whether the catalyst layer remains intact, whether the surface is flooded, whether
there is electrolyte residue, and whether any catalyst peeling or diminution is observed.
We check whether the membrane space is clean and uniformly wetted, whether there is any
electrolyte crossover, salt precipitation, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.
electrolyte crossover, salt precipitation, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.
electrolyte crossover, salt precipitation, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.
electrolyte crossover, salt precipitation, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.
We check whether the chamber is uniformly filled, whether there is any trapped gas bubble,
We check whether the chamber is uniformly filled, whether there is any trapped gas bubble,
or liquid stage gradation, salt carburization, or blockage near the inlet and outlet.
or liquid stage gradation, salt carburization, or blockage near the inlet and outlet.
or liquid stage gradation, salt carburization, or blockage near the inlet and outlet.
Then we inspect the cathode side membrane.
We check whether the membrane space is clean and uniformly wetted, and whether there is
electrolyte crossover, salt deposition, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.
electrolyte crossover, salt deposition, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.

electrolyte crossover, salt deposition, mechanical damage, or uneven contact.
Next, we inspect the cathode side electrode.
The cathode is CuSpR-CoT on a H23C3 carbon paper.

We check whether the catalyst layer remains attached, whether the cathode is flooded or dried out, whether there is any color change after reaction, and whether the active area
